title: From Spreadsheets to Insights: ​Turn Disconnected Skills Data into Intelligence
text:
See which roles AI will reshape next, and what skills your workforce needs now.

Get Your Report

Skills Data Enrichment

# From Spreadsheets to Insights: ​Turn Disconnected Skills Data into Intelligence

Outdated job roles and static skills frameworks can’t keep up with today’s rapid change. AI-powered skills data enrichment is the key to unlocking precision in workforce planning, sharper L&D investments, and talent agility.

Book a Demo

A professional smiling man with floating labels Skills-First, Precision, Mobility, Intelligence, and Agility.

## Too Much Data, Too Little Direction.

Organizations are overwhelmed with skills data trapped in legacy HR systems, scattered across business tools, tangled in outdated taxonomies and inconsistent job-role frameworks. This fragmented view stalls workforce agility and decision-making.

## Transform Skills Data with iMocha

iMocha empowers organizations to modernize their workforce architecture through enriched, AI-validated skills data.

Standardization That Scales

iMocha creates a consistent, enterprise-wide language of skills—bridging internal role complexity with globally recognized frameworks and structured enrichment.

.avif)

Role Clarity Through Capability Mapping

We connect every job profile to the right set of capabilities, skills, and proficiencies—creating clarity, consistency, and alignment across HR functions.

.avif)

Intelligence for Talent Agility

Using AI, iMocha surfaces adjacent and emerging skills—empowering proactive workforce shifts, smarter career growth, and responsive talent strategies.

## Enriched Skills Taxonomy

Standardize skills with a AI-powered taxonomy enrichment 

iMocha contextually enriches each skill with meaningful metadata to ensure consistency and depth across all roles and domains.

4-level structure: Domain » Sub-domain » Skill Clusters » Skills

Tagged with rubrics, certifications, and descriptive metadata

Based on O\*Net, Singapore Skills Framework, and industry-specific frameworks​

Skills taxonomy diagram showing hierarchy from occupation to job family, job role, capabilities, and skills.

Skills ontology diagram showing structured mapping of roles, capabilities, and skills.

## AI-powered Ontology & Capability Mapping​

Align job profiles with required skills and proficiencies 

Every job profile is mapped to relevant skills and capabilities, along with defined proficiency levels, enabling alignment with strategic workforce planning.​

Map job roles to capabilities and granular skill sets​

Define proficiency levels by skill to support decision-making​

Build a role-skills-capability graph that aligns with org goals​

## Stay Up-to-date with Discovered Skills

Reveal emerging and hidden skills across the workforce 

Our AI engine continuously identifies new, in-demand skills through multiple dynamic sources, keeping your skills data updated and future-ready.

Inferred from resumes, job profiles, adjacent skill sets​

Captured from employee inputs and real-time context​

Keeps your taxonomy responsive to business change​

iMocha taxonomy dashboard showing domains, sub-domains, skill clusters, and skills with filter options.

Adjacent skills mapping diagram showing relationships between related skills.

## Adjacent Skills Mapping

Activate workforce mobility through AI-driven contextual skill relationships 

By mapping skill adjacencies, iMocha empowers cross-functional deployment, enabling talent fluidity and agile project staffing.

Identify skill adjacencies across functions and domains

Unlock hidden talent pools for redeployment and growth​

Drive agile workforce planning through multidimensional insights​

## Ready to enrich your workforce with structured, AI-powered skills data?

Transform your skills data—with iMocha’s Skills Intelligence.

Book a Demo

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is Upskilling?

Upskilling is strengthening current skills to stay competitive in evolving roles. For example: A software developer learning cloud computing to build scalable, future-ready applications. 

### What is Reskilling?

Reskilling is equipping employees with new skills to shift into different roles. For example: A React developer trained in Angular to contribute across diverse front-end frameworks. 

### Why is upskilling and reskilling critical in today’s workplace?

As job roles evolve rapidly, organizations must continuously align employee capabilities with business needs. Upskilling and reskilling reduce hiring costs, boost retention, and ensure workforce agility.

### How does iMocha personalize learning for different employees?

iMocha uses real-time skills data and AI to benchmark current capabilities, identify gaps, and recommend learning paths tailored to each individual’s role, potential, and career goals.

### Can iMocha integrate with our existing LMS or LXP?

Yes. iMocha integrates seamlessly with leading LMS, LXP, and HR systems to trigger contextual learning experiences within your existing ecosystem.

### How can we measure the success of a reskilling program?

With iMocha, you can track skill development progress, readiness scores, engagement levels, and business impact using intuitive dashboards and analytics.

### FAQs for Skills Data Enrichment

### How does iMocha turn scattered skills data into actionable intelligence?

iMocha unifies fragmented data from resumes, HR systems, and employee inputs into a centralized skills ontology, enhanced with AI-enriched skills intelligence metadata, enabling structured workforce planning and agile decision-making.

### What is the structure of iMocha’s enriched skills taxonomy?

The taxonomy follows a 4-level hierarchy—Domain, Sub-domain, Skill Clusters, and Skills—tagged with rubrics, certifications, and metadata. It’s aligned with O\*Net, Singapore Skills Framework, and industry-specific models.

### How does iMocha keep our skills data future-ready and relevant?

iMocha’s AI engine continuously discovers and updates emerging skills by analyzing adjacent skills, resume, certification, employee request and job profile, ensuring taxonomies reflect real-time industry shifts.

### Can iMocha support agile workforce planning and cross-functional staffing?

Yes. Through adjacent skills mapping, iMocha reveals contextual relationships between skills, enabling rapid redeployment, project-based resourcing, and internal mobility across functions.

### How are job profiles aligned with capabilities in iMocha’s platform?

Each job profile is mapped to granular skill sets and required proficiency levels, forming a role-skills-capability graph that supports workforce strategy, L&D planning, and hiring benchmarks.

### How can iMocha help reduce redundancy and inconsistency in our current skills framework?

By standardizing and enriching skills with descriptive metadata, iMocha removes duplication, improves clarity, and brings consistency across departments and business tools.

### What role does iMocha play in identifying high-potential talent for new roles?

By uncovering hidden adjacencies and contextual skills, iMocha helps spot talent with transferable capabilities—ideal for lateral moves, cross-functional assignments, or leadership pathways.
